properties.remark: Clearfield ASOS reported visibility below a quarter of a mile, heavy snow, and a wind gust of near 31 mph.
Cambria County Airport ASOS reported less than a quarter of a mile visibility with heavy snow and wind gusts between 45-50 mph.
Rapid visibility drop due to heavy snowfall observed on webcams with RWIS sensor reporting visibility drops and snow-covered roadways.
Visibility dropped below a quarter of a mile with heavy snowfall observed on webcam and RWIS data network. Observed at the Penfield exit of Interstate 80.
Blair County Airport ASOS reported visibility dropping below a quarter of a mile with heavy snow and wind gusts between 35 and 40 mph.
Visibility dropped below a quarter of a mile with heavy snowfall observed on RWIS data and webcams.
Observed heavy snowfall and lowered visibility on RWIS data and webcams.
Drops in visibility observed on RWIS webcam with heavy snowfall covering the roadways in a five to ten minute timespan.
Visibility drop and heavy snow observed on RWIS data and webcams.
PADOT RWIS observed sharp drop in vis to 0.2 miles or 1191ft with heavy burst of snow.
PADOT RWIS SR 54 W/B Natalie Mtn observed sharp drop is vis to near 0.25 mile with heavy burst of snow.

Road surface temperature dropped below freezing, level of grip dropped below 0.30, and visibility dropped below one quarter mile (<1000 ft). No impacts were reported and roads remained wet due to chemical treatment.

Visibility dropped to 1/3 mile. Road surface temperatures dropped to 33.4F at this site, but dropped below freezing at the site just across the Centre/Clearfield line. No impacts were reported. Roads were slushy at the site across the county line and remained wet due to chemical treatment at this RWIS site.

This RWIS site was down at the time the snow squall moved through, so unfortunately specific observations are unavailable. Webcams showed roadways became completely snow covered, though no traffic incidents occurred. Road surface temperatures dropped to 32.7F at the RWIS a few miles southeast of this site and below freezing at a site farther northwest, indicating temperatures likely dropped to or below freezing here.
Road surface temperature dropped below freezing and visibility dropped to one quarter mile (1345 ft). No impacts were reported and roads briefly became slushy (11:50-12:00).
Road surface temperature dropped below freezing, level of grip dropped below 0.5, and visibility dropped below one quarter mile (<1000 ft). No impacts were reported and roads became snow-covered.
Visibility less than 1/4 mile as viewed on the webcam at Plank Road.
Visibility less than 1/4 mile as observed via the webcam at Cresson Summit and the Prince Gallizin webcam sites.

RWIS site on Interstate 80 at Penfield recorded rapid visibility drops below a quarter of a mile with heavy snow observed on webcam. Road surface temperatures dropped 7 degrees in 20 minutes but stayed above freezing.

Corrects previous snow squall report from Penfield. RWIS site reported visibility less than 1/4 mile in heavy snow, road surface temperature 31.4F and air temperature 28F during snow squall. Corrected LAT/LON 41.10627 -78.53456 (Penfield 8 SSE).
Clearfield ASOS reported surface visbility 1/4 mile in heavy snow, Concurrent RWIS road surface temperatures below freezing at I-80 exits 111 and 120.

*** 2 INJ *** Corrects time on previous snow squall report from 1 E Carroll. RWIS data indicated a rapid drop in visibility to a quarter of a mile with roadways becoming snow covered quickly in a snow squall. Air and surface temperatures were already below freezing before the snow squall, but data indicated a 4 degree drop in surface temperatures during the squall. This squall resulted in a multi vehicle incident on Interstate 80 involving 10 tractor trailers and 3 to 4 passenger vehicles, closing the roadway for multiple hours.

*** 2 INJ *** RWIS data indicated a rapid drop in visibility to a quarter of a mile with roadways becoming snow covered quickly in a snow squall. Air and surface temperatures were already below freezing before the snow squall, but data indicated a 4 degree drop in surface temperatures during the squall. This squall resulted in a multi vehicle incident on Interstate 80 involving 10 tractor trailers and 3 to 4 passenger vehicles, closing the roadway for multiple hours.

RWIS site reported a 2 to 3 degree drop in surface temperatures along with visibility dropping to one quarter mile within a snow squall. Webcams in the surrounding area also showed accumulating snow on roadways in the squall for a period of 5 to 10 minutes.
RWIS site on Interstate 80 reported a drop to a quarter of a mile with heavy snow in the squall allowing for accumulating snow covering roadways.
